news.headline: Sonic Labs shifts from USD algorithmic stablecoin to UAE dirham alternative after criticism. UAE dirham CBDC set for Q4 2025 launch.
news.summary: Sonic Labs has scrapped plans for a US dollar-pegged algorithmic stablecoin in favor of developing a UAE dirham-denominated alternative. Initially expected to have a 23% APR, this decision came after criticism and concerns over algorithmic stablecoins following the collapse of the $40 billion Terra ecosystem. The UAE will launch its digital dirham CBDC in Q4 2025, aiming to enhance financial stability and combat financial crime. The shift in strategy reflects a broader trend of regulatory scrutiny over stablecoins and highlights the increasing use of stablecoins for daily transactions rather than large transfers. The MiCA bill in the EU will prohibit algorithmic stablecoins to prevent similar failures.

news.headline: JASPER in Nashville marks 5 years of excellence in luxury home building and renovation, prioritizing precision and integrity.
news.summary: JASPER, a leading luxury builder in Nashville, marks an important milestone as they commemorate their fifth year of creating bespoke homes and innovative spaces with meticulous attention to detail, honesty, and a dedication to prioritizing client needs. Recently celebrated in Nashville, Tennessee on March 29, 2025 . JASPER has established itself as a top custom home builder and expert in luxury renovations.

news.headline: ConcertAI's TeraRecon and 3DR Labs partnering to enhance AI-enabled image post-processing for US healthcare.
news.summary: In Cambridge, Massachusetts and Louisville, Kentucky, ConcertAI's TeraRecon, a prominent figure in combining advanced imaging procedures with clinical AI, has disclosed an extended collaboration with 3DR® Labs, a top supplier of 3D medical image post-processing and incorporated AI services in the U.S. This partnership seeks to elevate clinical service provision for advanced imaging by broadening the scope of domain-specific workflows and clinical AI technologies. The collaboration anticipates delivering more comprehensive services utilizing cutting-edge technologies for improved patient care.

news.headline: Eoptolink debuts Gen2 1.6T OSFP transceivers at OFC 2025. Leading provider showcases advanced technology.
news.summary: In San Francisco on March 29, 2025, Eoptolink Technology Inc., Ltd., a prominent company listed on SZSE as 300502, will showcase its latest innovation - the second iteration of fully retimed 1.6T OSFP transceivers at OFC 2025. This technology demonstration illustrates the company's commitment to providing cutting-edge optical transceiver solutions. This advancement marks a significant step forward in the industry's drive for enhanced communication capabilities.

news.headline: Eoptolink debuts 1.6T OSFP 2VR4 transceivers with 200G VCSELs, operating at 212Gb/s per lambda at OFC 2025.
news.summary: In San Francisco on March 29, 2025, Eoptolink Technology Inc., Ltd. (SZSE: 300502), a prominent supplier of cutting-edge optical transceiver solutions, is showcasing its latest 1.6T multimode transceiver at the OFC 2025 event. This innovative transceiver operates at an impressive speed of 212Gb/s per lambda, demonstrating the company's commitment to pushing the boundaries of optical communication technology.

news.headline: Omega World Travel celebrates women's leadership year-round, hosting events like Entrepreneurs of Purpose retreat dinner on March 8th at Watergate Hotel.
news.summary: In Fairfax, Virginia, on March 29, 2025, Omega World Travel demonstrated its commitment to empowering women by celebrating Women's History Month. The company's support extends beyond just one month, as evidenced by their hosting of the Entrepreneurs of Purpose retreat dinner on March 8th at the iconic Watergate Hotel. Omega's dedication to advancing women's causes shines through in events like these, showcasing their ongoing efforts to empower women in their community. The event was attended by a diverse group of women, with the aim of fostering connections and inspiring leadership. Omega's initiatives highlight their commitment to supporting and uplifting women in all aspects of life.

news.headline: KIIT Deemed to be University in Bhubaneswar, India expands global presence via strategic collaborations with international institutions.Characters: 119
news.summary: In March 29, 2025, the well-known KIIT Deemed to be University in Bhubaneswar, India, has been making significant strides in broadening its international presence by forming strategic collaborations with reputable global institutions. The university has recently inked Memoranda of Understanding (MoUs) as part of its continued expansion efforts.

news.headline: 25% tariff unsettles Canadian auto industry as Trump surprises country during election campaign.
news.summary: The U.S. president surprised Canada by imposing a 25 percent tariff on the auto industry, causing disruption and uncertainty.

news.headline: Stablecoin supply may hit $1T by 2025, possibly igniting crypto market growth. Currently, stablecoins near $208B ATH. Payments using stablecoins also rising, showing growth in blockchain transactions.
news.summary: The global stablecoin supply is projected to reach $1 trillion by the end of 2025, with a potential surge from $225 billion to $1 trillion just within the current year. CoinFund managing partner David Pakman anticipates this surge to be a key catalyst for broader cryptocurrency market growth, particularly benefiting decentralized finance (DeFi) activity. The aggregate stablecoin supply hit an all-time high of over $208 billion across the top five stablecoins in March 28. The market is still considered mid-cycle, as the stablecoin supply continues to rise. Stablecoin usage for daily payments is increasing, with a significant increase in volume since 2021, indicating a shift towards using stablecoins for payments rather than just large transfers. However, stablecoin supply alone may not significantly impact Bitcoin's price without additional catalysts, according to experts.

news.headline: Deactivated from gig jobs devastates millions financially as Uber or DoorDash accounts blocked abruptly.
news.summary: Large numbers of Americans generate income by securing gig opportunities on popular platforms such as Uber, Lyft, and DoorDash. A significant portion of these individuals face financial hardship when their accounts are unexpectedly suspended for reasons that are often ambiguous.

news.headline: Bloomberg faces challenges with A.I. summaries, issuing multiple corrections to news summaries using the technology this year.
news.summary: The news organization has made corrections to over 50 A.I.-generated news summaries since implementing the technology for writing them earlier this year.

news.headline: Pittsburgh Airport uses on-site natural gas & solar power to prevent outages & cut energy expenses, unlike Heathrow.
news.summary: Pittsburgh International Airport has successfully avoided power outages and decreased energy expenses by producing electricity on its premises through a combination of natural gas and solar power. This initiative has allowed the airport to become more self-sufficient, with significant reductions in their reliance on external energy sources. The implementation of this energy generation strategy has not only ensured uninterrupted power supply but has also resulted in notable cost savings for the airport.

news.headline: Trump criticizes law firms for filing "frivolous" lawsuits, potentially impacting Americans' right to sue government.
news.summary: The president criticized law firms for engaging in what he termed as "frivolous" lawsuits. However, his actions may threaten the fundamental right of Americans to file legal claims against their government.

news.headline: In Zambia, an initiative is demonstrating how climate-resilient chickens can aid farmers in combating poverty with increased egg production.
news.summary: A company in Zambia is demonstrating that it is possible for a profit-driven enterprise to assist rural farmers dealing with severe weather conditions in breeding chickens that produce a higher number of eggs.

news.headline: NAYG lawsuit against Galaxy called "lawfare" by Scaramucci over Terra promotion. Settlement: $200 million. Martin Act scrutinized for lowering standard of proof. Galaxy accused of promoting LUNA tokens without disclosure, profiting hundreds of millions. Scaramucci defends Novogratz, blames Terraform Labs for deception. Terra's collapse noted as infamous in crypto industry.
news.summary: The New York State Attorney General (NAYG) has taken legal action against Galaxy Digital over ties to Terra (LUNA) cryptocurrency, citing violations of the Martin Act, a strict anti-fraud law. Galaxy Digital settled for $200 million, accused of acquiring LUNA tokens at a discount and promoting them without disclosure. SkyBridge Capital's Anthony Scaramucci criticized the Martin Act, stating it allows for abuse. The case alleges Galaxy helped raise LUNA's market price from $0.31 to $119.18, making hundreds of millions. The collapse of Terra in 2022 was referred to as a "house of cards" in the crypto world. Industry figures like Keith Grossman and Anthony Pompliano expressed their views on the situation, supporting Galaxy CEO Michael Novogratz.

news.headline: Private schools facing challenges due to Labour's tax plan as backlash grows against the proposal.
Death toll in South Korea fire reaches 30; Govt commits to providing relief efforts for the victims.
Controversy brews as Labour Party's tax plan for private schools faces scrutiny, with support wavering.
ADC Submarine Cable launched in Hong Kong on March 24, 2025, connecting over 100 guests for a digital future.
news.summary: In Hong Kong on March 24, 2025, the commissioning launch of ADC Submarine Cable took place at the Everbright Centre. More than 100 distinguished guests attended the event, including notable political figures.

news.headline: LONGi Chairman Zhong Baoshen promotes China-Australia tech collab in clean energy at Boao Forum for Asia.Key info: Boao Forum for Asia 2025 held from March 25 to 28 in Boao, Hainan, China.
news.summary: The Boao Forum for Asia 2025 Annual Conference took place in Boao, Hainan, China from March 25 to 28. The theme of the conference was "Asia in the changing World: Towards a shared Future." Zhong Baoshen, the Chairman of LONGi, was among the attendees at the event.

news.headline: ETH's investment appeal dwindles due to greedy L2s. ETH at $1,894, down 5.34% in 7 days. ETH/BTC ratio at 0.02260.
news.summary: The declining appeal of Ether (ETH) as an investment is attributed to layer-2 networks draining value from the main network and excessive token creation, according to Castle Island Ventures partner Nic Carter. This sentiment was echoed by Lekker Capital founder Quinn Thompson, who called Ether "completely dead" as an investment due to a $225 billion market cap network witnessing declines in transaction activity, user growth, and fees. The ETH/BTC ratio is at its lowest level in nearly five years at 0.02260. Ether is currently trading at $1,894, down 5.34% in the past seven days and 17.94% in the past 30 days. Ethereum's fee revenue reportedly dropped by 99% over six months in September 2024 as L2s absorbed users and revenue. Despite previous optimism of Ether reaching $10,000 in 2025, projections have been revised down, with Standard Chartered forecasting a price of $4,000 by the end of 2025. However, some traders remain bullish on Ether's potential.

news.headline: Transistor breakthrough by NUS team led by Prof. Lanza transforms neuromorphic computing; redefines building block for AI.
news.summary: On March 28, 2025, in Singapore, an innovative breakthrough in neuromorphic computing was achieved by a team headed by Associate Professor Mario Lanza from the Department of Materials Science and Engineering in the College of Design and Engineering at the National University of Singapore. This advancement marks a significant milestone in this field.

news.headline: BRI holds AGMS, distributes IDR 51.73 trillion in dividends, plans IDR 3 trillion buyback.
news.summary: In Jakarta, Indonesia on March 29, 2025, PT Bank Rakyat Indonesia (Persero) Tbk (IDX: BBRI) conducted its Annual General Meeting of Shareholders (AGMS). During the meeting, BRI announced the approval of dividend distribution totaling IDR 51.73 trillion. This marked an increase from the previous year's dividends distributed in 2024.

news.headline: Zylox-Tonbridge achieves first full-year profit in 2024 with net profit over USD 14 million, revenue hits USD 109.4 million.
news.summary: In Hangzhou, China, on March 28, 2025, Zylox-Tonbridge Medical Technology Co., Ltd. (Stock Code: 2190.HK) disclosed its 2024 annual financial outcomes. During the year, the Company generated a revenue of $109.4 million.

news.headline: Q4 2024 financial results released, showing a 10% increase in revenue and a net profit of $1.5 million.
news.summary: Last week, it was reported that there has been a significant increase in the number of COVID-19 cases in the country. The latest data shows that there were over 10,000 new confirmed cases, bringing the total number of cases to more than 150,000. Additionally, the death toll has risen to over 2,500. Authorities are urging the public to adhere to safety guidelines and protocols to prevent further spread of the virus. In response to the surge in cases, the government has implemented stricter measures, including lockdowns in highly affected areas. Health officials are working tirelessly to provide care for those affected and to ramp up testing and vaccination efforts. The situation is being closely monitored, and updates will be provided as the situation develops.

news.headline: Trump faces setbacks in retaliation against law firms, with rulings blocking punishments in executive orders. No ban on lawyers from govt locations, meetings, or positions.
news.summary: The recent court rulings have prevented the administration from enforcing penalties outlined in the executive orders, such as prohibiting their attorneys from accessing government facilities, attending meetings, or securing employment opportunities.

news.headline: Former Louisiana AG initiates investigation into Sage Therapeutics, Inc. for potential misconduct;  Lawsuit by Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC.
news.summary: On March 28, 2025, in New York and New Orleans, Charles C. Foti, Jr., a former Attorney General of Louisiana and current partner at Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC, revealed an ongoing investigation into Sage Therapeutics, Inc. The investigation by KSF, has begun regarding potential misconduct by the Company, seeking to uncover any irregularities that may impact stakeholders. Sage Therapeutics, a notable entity in the pharmaceutical industry, is under scrutiny for undisclosed reasons that have prompted this examination. The probe aims to shed light on any potential violations or breaches that could affect the business operations and financial standing of Sage Therapeutics, impacting its investors and the market as a whole. Charles C. Foti, Jr. and his team are dedicated to uncovering the truth behind this situation, with specific numbers and details yet to be disclosed to the public as the investigation proceeds.

news.headline: Former Louisiana Attorney General initiates investigation into Chipotle Mexican Grill. Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC to scrutinize officers and directors of CMG.
news.summary: On March 28, 2025, in New York and New Orleans, Charles C. Foti, Jr., the former Attorney General of Louisiana, who is now a partner at Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC ("KSF"), reported the initiation of an investigation by KSF into Chipotle Mexican Grill, Inc. The investigation focuses on potential irregularities within the Company.

news.headline: Firm Skadden Arps avoids executive order with $100 million pro bono work for Trump-supported issues, says president.
news.summary: The president announced that the law firm Skadden, Arps, Slate, Meagher & Flom will be offering $100 million worth of pro bono services towards causes that align with his priorities.

news.headline: US vaccine official steps down due to RFK misinformation, following a report from Bloomberg.
Sherritt confirms support from noteholders for CBCA Transaction to extend maturities and strengthen capital structure. TSX:S.
news.summary: Toronto-based company Sherritt International Corporation provided an update on the level of support from Noteholders regarding their plan to extend the maturities of the Corporation’s note obligations and enhance its capital structure through the CBCA Transaction. This transaction aims to be executed under a corporate plan of arrangement in compliance with the Canada Business Corporations Act. Sherritt disclosed that the Noteholders, as defined earlier, have shown significant support for the initiative, signifying a positive response from stakeholders. The corporation, listed on the Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX:S), is progressing with the transaction to fortify its financial standing and ensure long-term sustainability.

news.headline: Junshi Biosciences discloses 2024 financial results; an innovative biopharmaceutical company with updates.
news.summary: Shanghai-based Junshi Biosciences Co., Ltd, a prominent biopharmaceutical company (HKEX: 1877; SSE: 688180), unveiled its financial outcomes for the entire year of 2024. Specializing in pioneering therapies, the company disclosed its corporate updates on March 28, 2025.

news.headline: VOA journalists to receive career support amid tough times after Trump administration's decision to end federal grant funding, leading to shutdown and furlough of all staff.
news.summary: On March 28, 2025, in Washington, dedicated journalists at Voice of America faced difficulties due to the Trump Administration’s choice to halt federal grant funding to the U.S. Agency for Global Media. This caused VOA to shut down and placed all employees on furlough.

news.headline: Eastern Platinum Limited reported a decrease in revenue to $17.0M in Q4 2024 and $62.5M for FY2024, operating income and net loss also decreased significantly for both periods. Working capital deficit was $38.7M in Dec 2024. CEO aims to improve production and profits in 2025.
news.summary: Eastern Platinum Limited reported a decrease in revenue for Q4 2024 to $17.0 million (a -44.3% decrease from Q4 2023) and for FY2024 to $62.5 million (a -41.5% decrease from FY2023). Mine operating income showed a loss of -$7.9 million in Q4 2024, a significant decrease from $7.8 million in Q4 2023, with a gross margin of -46.2%. Operating income was -$12.7 million in FY2024, compared to $18.5 million in FY2023, marking a -168.6% decrease. Net loss attributable to shareholders was -$11.9 million in Q4 2024 and -$12.8 million in FY2024, from $3.3 million and $13.8 million in Q4 2023 and FY2023, respectively. The Company had a working capital deficit of $38.7 million as at December 31, 2024, with short-term cash resources of $3.1 million. The CEO expressed confidence in profitability, aiming to improve production results while keeping costs in check, expecting a rebound in PGM and chrome prices in 2025. Chrome concentrate sales contributed significantly to revenue, along with PGM sales under an agreement with Impala Platinum Limited.

news.headline: Former South Korean president Moon is summoned to be questioned about corruption allegations: Yonhap reports.
Turnstone Biologics Corp. reveals financial results for Q4 and FY 2024.

Elon Musk sells X to xAI in all-stock deal, facing lawsuit due to class-action accusations. Billion-dollar acquisition raises concerns. Musk values xAI at $80B and X at $33B; deal intertwines their futures. Musk's AI entity exposed. Musk pays premium over X value, stirring investor disputes. Grok chatbot at center of $80B valuation scrutiny. Elon Musk faces lawsuit amidst xAI-X deal.
news.summary: Elon Musk has sold his social media platform X to his AI startup xAI in an all-stock deal valued at $80 billion for xAI and $33 billion for X. The acquisition coincides with a US judge rejecting Musk’s bid to dismiss a lawsuit accusing him of defrauding former Twitter shareholders by delaying disclosure of his initial investment. Musk originally bought X for $44 billion in April 2022. The deal merges xAI and X to combine data, models, and talent to accelerate human progress. However, critics like Adam Cochran claim the transaction has overvalued X and is detrimental to xAI and X investors. Musk's AI chatbot Grok, integral to X, has been valued at $80 billion, with differing opinions on its worth. While Musk emphasizes using AI to benefit humanity, Cochran criticizes the deal as detrimental to investors and potentially selling user data to xAI.

news.headline: Ai4, the largest AI industry event in the U.S., sold to CloserStill Media, advised by JEGI CLARITY.
news.summary: On March 28, 2025, Ai4, under the guidance of JEGI CLARITY, was acquired by CloserStill Media. Ai4 is recognized as the leading AI event in the United States, hosting the foremost cross-industry conference and exhibition for companies leveraging AI for innovative purposes. This acquisition marks a significant development in the industry landscape, with Ai4's prominence and influence expected to continue under the new ownership.

Starlink to offer earthquake relief kits in Myanmar, Thailand.

news.summary: On March 28, 2025, in Vancouver, BC, WELL Health Technologies Corp. (TSX: WELL) (OTCQX: WHTCF), a digital healthcare company, aimed to improve health outcomes by utilizing technology to empower healthcare providers and patients.

news.headline: Women’s March Madness ratings drop without Caitlin Clark but there’s no cause for alarm.
US LBM appoints Jonathan Greene as Chief Digital & Technology Officer. Greene to transition from Chief Digital Officer.
news.summary: A major distributor of specialty building materials in the United States, US LBM, based in Atlanta, revealed new leadership on March 28, 2025. Jonathan Greene, the current Chief Digital Officer, is set to assume a new position within the company. US LBM is known for its wide reach and significant market presence within the country.

news.headline: Vice President J.D. Vance and Waltz set to join tour of Greenland, adding to Balance of Power.
DriveTime teams up with Girl Gang Garage to donate a refurbished van to Maggie's Place in Phoenix on March 26.
news.summary: Phoenix, in a press release dated March 28, 2025, DriveTime announced their upcoming donation event scheduled for Saturday, March 26 at 10 a.m. The company, alongside Girl Gang Garage, will be gifting a completely renovated van to Maggie's Place as part of their ongoing community support initiatives. This charitable act represents DriveTime's dedication to giving back to the community.

news.headline: Sunnova adopts tax asset preservation plan to protect $1.4 billion in NOLs, ensuring long-term shareholder value.
news.summary: Houston-based company Sunnova Energy International Inc., traded under ticker symbol NOVA on NYSE, revealed their implementation of a shareholder rights plan on Monday. The plan aims to safeguard long-term shareholder interests by safeguarding Sunnova's net operating loss carryforwards and other tax attributes under the Internal Revenue Code. As of the end of 2024, Sunnova possessed around $1.4 billion worth of U.S. federal NOLs, set to remain available for utilization with the new Tax Asset Preservation Plan in place.

news.headline: Partners Value Investments Inc. releases 2024 annual results in U.S. dollars.
news.summary: Toronto-based Partners Value Investments Inc., known as "the Company," released its financial results for the year ending on December 31, 2024. The company trades on the Toronto Stock Exchange under the tickers TSX: PVF.WT and PVF.PR.V. All financial figures are denominated in U.S. dollars as per the announcement made on March 28, 2025.

news.headline: Partners Value Investments L.P. reports 2024 annual results in U.S. dollars on March 28, 2025 (TSX: PVF.UN TSX:PVF.PR.U).
news.summary: On March 28, 2025, Partners Value Investments L.P. (referred to as the “Partnership” and listed as TSX: PVF.UN and TSX: PVF.PR.U) released its financial results for the year ending December 31, 2024, with all figures denominated in U.S. dollars.

news.headline: HYTN qualifies 4000kg cannabis for German market through supply agreements, growing international presence.
news.summary: In Vancouver, British Columbia, on March 28, 2025, HYTN Innovations Inc. (CSE: HYTN, FSE: 85W0, OTC PINK: HYTNF) - a prominent pharmaceutical-grade cannabis manufacturer - has confirmed its recent entry into strategic supply agreements and successful completion of vendor qualification with two cannabis cultivators. This move, under the Company's Good Manufacturing Practice (GMP) system, secures a minimum of 4000 kg of cannabis for the German market. This development follows HYTN's prior announcements regarding its shift towards international pharmaceutical sales, which notably included partnerships with SNDL Inc (SNDL) and 4C Labs. These initiatives not only enhance HYTN’s manufacturing capabilities but also position the Company for further global expansion.

news.headline: Partners Value Split Corp. reports 2024 annual results, with net asset value per unit at $171.41, up from $124.10 in 2023.
news.summary: On March 28, 2025, Partners Value Split Corp. disclosed that the net asset value per unit stood at $171.41 as of December 31, 2024, a significant increase from $124.10 recorded on December 31, 2023. Trading under the symbols TSX: PVS.PR.G, PVS.PR.H, PVS.PR.I, PVS.PR.J, PVS.PR.K, and PVS.PR.L, the Company reported all figures in U.S. dollars.
